pairs.question: Who was Arthur Walker Brady and what political roles did he hold in Scotland?
pairs.answer: Arthur Walker Brady was a Scottish politician who served as a member of the Independent Labour Party (ILP), was elected to Irvine Town Council and Ayrshire County Council (where he chaired the Labour Party group), and became the treasurer of the Scottish Council of the ILP. He later co-founded the Scottish Socialist Party (SSP) and served as its general secretary before becoming Chairman of the Scottish Labour Party.

pairs.question: What was Arthur Walker Brady's involvement in the Scottish Socialist Party (SSP)?
pairs.answer: Brady co-founded the SSP with Patrick Dollan and other ILP MPs, aiming to continue ILP policies while re-affiliating with the Labour Party. He served as the group's general secretary and successfully affiliated it to Labour, representing it on the Scottish Council of Labour until the SSP dissolved in 1940.

pairs.question: Why did Arthur Walker Brady leave the Independent Labour Party (ILP) in 1931?
pairs.answer: Brady was part of the group that disagreed with the ILP's policy of disaffiliating from the Labour Party in 1931. This disagreement led to his involvement in forming the Scottish Socialist Party (SSP), which sought to re-affiliate with Labour while maintaining ILP policies.

pairs.question: What were Arthur Walker Brady's electoral results in the 1935 general election?
pairs.answer: In the 1935 general election, Brady represented the Labour Party in Ayr Burghs and received 13,274 votes, but he did not win the seat.

pairs.question: What were the key political positions held by Arthur Walker Brady in Glasgow during the 1930s and 1940s?
pairs.answer: Brady moved to Glasgow to administer the SSP and was elected Secretary of the Glasgow Trades and Labour Council in 1937. In 1942, after Labour split the posts, he became the secretary of the Glasgow Burgh Labour Party, resigning from the trades council role.

pairs.question: How did Arthur Walker Brady contribute to the Labour Party's organizational structure in Scotland?
pairs.answer: Brady served on the Scottish Council of Labour after affiliating the SSP to Labour, and later became Chairman of the Scottish Labour Party. His roles included representing Labour in local councils, trade unions, and party administrative bodies.

pairs.question: What was the relationship between the Scottish Socialist Party (SSP) and the Labour Party during Arthur Walker Brady's leadership?
pairs.answer: The SSP, led by Brady, sought to continue ILP policies while re-affiliating to the Labour Party. Brady successfully affiliated the SSP to Labour and represented it on the Scottish Council of Labour, ensuring close ties between the two organizations.
